steps.title: Corresponding angles in similar triangles are congruent
steps.content: Since triangle $XYZ$ is similar to triangle $RST$ with the correspondence $Z \leftrightarrow T$, we apply the fundamental property of similar figures: corresponding angles are congruent. Therefore, $\angle T = \angle Z$.

steps.title: Substitute the given angle measure
steps.content:
We're given that $\angle Z = 20^{\circ}$, so $\angle T = 20^{\circ}$.

Note: The condition $2XY = RS$ specifies that side lengths are in a ratio of $1:2$ (the scale factor), but angles in similar triangles are always equal regardless of size. The scale factor affects side lengths and areas, not angles.

C is correct.
